title: Jalebi

ingredients.title: For the batter
items: ¼ tsp turmeric powder (for color, optional)
½ cup water (adjust to get a thick but flowing batter)

ingredients.title: For frying
items: Ghee or refined oil (for deep frying)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Batter
steps: Mix maida, cornflour, turmeric, and baking powder in a bowl.
Add yogurt and water gradually to form a smooth, thick batter (like pancake batter but slightly thicker).
Cover and ferment for 8–12 hours at room temperature (or 4–5 hours in a warm place).
After fermentation, stir gently. If the batter is too thick, add a few tablespoons of water.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Sugar Syrup
steps: In a pan, combine sugar and water. Heat until the sugar dissolves completely.
Boil for 5–7 minutes until it reaches one-string consistency.
Tip: Test by taking a drop between your fingers; it should form a thin, single thread.
Add saffron strands and cardamom powder. Stir well.
Add lemon juice to prevent crystallization. Keep the syrup warm.

instruction.title: Step 3: Fry the Jalebis
steps: Heat ghee or refined oil in a wide pan over medium heat (not smoking hot).
Fill a piping bag or squeeze bottle with the batter.
Pipe spirals or pretzel shapes directly into the hot oil.
Fry until golden and crisp on both sides. Remove and drain excess oil on paper towels.

instruction.title: Step 4: Soak in Sugar Syrup
steps: Immediately dip the fried jalebis in warm sugar syrup for 30–60 seconds.
Remove and let them drain briefly on a plate.

chefTips: Fermentation: The secret to soft, fluffy insides is proper fermentation. Don’t skip it.
Oil temperature: Medium heat ensures crispy outside and cooked inside. Too hot = brown outside, raw inside.
Sugar syrup: Must be warm, not hot, when adding jalebis; this allows proper absorption.
Serving: Serve hot for the perfect texture and shine.
